dominators: Is it guaranteed that whenever line 7:  <statement> do { </statement> is reached, the statement line 9:  <statement> if ( p == NULL) { ret = -1; break; } </statement> has already been reached?
Is it guaranteed that whenever line 15:  <statement> } while ( 0 ); </statement> is reached, the statement line 11:  <statement> for (i=0; i<n; i++) { </statement> has already been reached?

Does variable ret at line 10: <statement> 		if ( n < 0 ) { ret = -1; break; } </statement> determine the value stored in variable i at line 11: <statement> 		for (i=0; i<n; i++) { </statement>?
liveness: Does variable 'i' remain in use at the end of function 'func'?
Is variable 'ret' live at the end of function 'func'?
dead_code: Can the program actually reach the statement: “int *p = NULL;”?
